description: Technology Risk & Control Analytics Platform — unify evidence→classification→control-tests→analytics pipeline, warehouse persistence, risk API, and executive dashboard while preserving governance boundaries (no autonomous remediation, dry-run default, proof tiers T0–T4).

safetyBoundaries: No process kill or silent registry mutation
proxy-disable and remediation remain dry-run by default
Listener/process attribution is correlation-only unless T4 writer proof
Classification is not accusation — no malware verdicts

userStories.title: Ralph workflow bootstrap (AGENTS.md + progress.txt)
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want Ralph workflow documentation and a progress log template so autonomous iterations preserve governance context across fresh agent sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: AGENTS.md includes Ralph loop instructions, safety boundaries, and verification commands
progress.txt exists with header: branch, date, story checklist
Documents that each story must not expand scope beyond its acceptance criteria
testPlan: Manual: verify AGENTS.md references prd.json and progress.txt paths
CI unchanged: full pytest suite still green
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Revert AGENTS.md and delete progress.txt; no runtime impact.
auditRequirements: No audit trail mutation — documentation-only story
Record story completion in progress.txt with date and agent notes
userStories.governanceImpact: Positive — encodes non-negotiable safety boundaries for autonomous agents.

userStories.title: Create platform_kernel package scaffold
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want a dedicated platform_kernel package so pipeline orchestration has one canonical home separate from CLI and API adapters.
acceptanceCriteria: platform_kernel/README.md documents purpose and non-goals (not remediation executor)
Package importable without side effects: python -c "import platform_kernel"
testPlan: tests/platform_kernel/test_package_import.py — import smoke test
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Remove platform_kernel/ directory entirely.
auditRequirements: No production audit JSONL changes
Document package boundary in progress.txt
userStories.riskImpact: Low — empty scaffold, no behavior change.
userStories.governanceImpact: Establishes canonical orchestration namespace for future consolidation.

userStories.title: Unified PipelineResult dataclass in platform_kernel
userStories.description: As an API consumer, I want a single typed PipelineResult so CLI, FastAPI, and analytics export share identical incident pipeline output shape.
acceptanceCriteria: platform_kernel/models.py defines PipelineResult with evidence, classification, control_tests, policy, limitations fields
Fields are JSON-serializable via .to_dict() or dataclass asdict
Includes explicit limitations[] on every result
testPlan: tests/platform_kernel/test_models.py — serialize/deserialize round-trip
Assert limitations key always present (may be empty list)
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Delete platform_kernel/models.py and revert __init__ exports.
auditRequirements: PipelineResult must not embed execution authority flags without policy_decision field
limitations[] required for governance traceability
userStories.riskImpact: Low — types only, no runtime pipeline change yet.
userStories.governanceImpact: Standardizes decision output for audit export and human review queues.

userStories.title: Implement run_incident_pipeline in platform_kernel
userStories.description: As a risk analyst, I want one orchestrator that chains evidence normalization, incident classification, control tests, and dashboard dataset building from fixture or audit JSONL input.
acceptanceCriteria: platform_kernel/pipeline.py exposes run_incident_pipeline(*, fixture, audit_path, dry_run=True)
dry_run=True is default; no registry or proxy mutation
testPlan: tests/platform_kernel/test_pipeline.py using tests/fixtures/analytics_pipeline_fixture.json
Assert incident count, control test IDs, and KPI keys match existing analytics_pipeline tests
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Remove platform_kernel/pipeline.py; existing analytics_pipeline CLI paths unchanged.
auditRequirements: Pipeline must not append audit records when dry_run=True unless explicitly passed audit_path for read-only ingest
Output includes proof_tier and limitations from classifier
userStories.governanceImpact: Single entry point reduces divergent pipeline interpretations across teams.

userStories.title: Golden fixture contract test for unified pipeline
userStories.description: As an SRE, I want a CI golden-file test so pipeline output schema cannot drift silently between releases.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/platform_kernel/test_pipeline_golden.py compares stable subset of PipelineResult JSON
Test documents which keys are intentionally excluded (timestamps, host-specific)
testPlan: pytest tests/platform_kernel/test_pipeline_golden.py
Update golden only with explicit reviewer approval noted in PR
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Delete golden fixture and test file; pipeline wrapper remains.
auditRequirements: Golden file must not contain real hostnames, registry paths, or PII
Use tests/fixtures/ synthetic data only
userStories.governanceImpact: Prevents regression in control-test mapping and incident classification labels.

userStories.title: Warehouse DDL — control_tests table
userStories.description: As a data engineer, I want a control_tests table in the analytics warehouse so mature control test results persist for Power BI and executive dashboards.
acceptanceCriteria: schemas/analytics_warehouse.sql adds control_tests table with: test_id, incident_id, control_id, status, evidence_tier, tested_at, limitations
testPlan: tests/schemas/test_warehouse_ddl.py — parse SQL and assert control_tests exists
Optional: apply DDL to in-memory SQLite in test
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Revert schemas/analytics_warehouse.sql and docs change; no data loss if table was never populated.
auditRequirements: control_tests.status must use enum values: PASS, FAIL, PARTIAL, NOT_TESTED, INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE
limitations column required (TEXT, nullable)
userStories.governanceImpact: Enables durable control-maturity reporting for risk committees.

userStories.title: Warehouse DDL — risk_decisions and risk_register tables
userStories.description: As a compliance officer, I want risk_decisions and risk_register tables so human-reviewed RiskDecisionRecord entries can be queried without parsing JSONL.
acceptanceCriteria: schemas/analytics_warehouse.sql adds risk_decisions (decision_id, incident_id, risk_rating, reviewer, decided_at, rollback_required, limitations)
schemas/analytics_warehouse.sql adds risk_register (register_id, domain, risk_statement, owner, review_due, status)
testPlan: tests/schemas/test_warehouse_ddl.py extended for new tables and indexes
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Revert DDL additions; existing incidents/evidence tables unaffected.
auditRequirements: risk_decisions.rollback_required defaults TRUE
No autonomous approval fields — reviewer must be nullable TEXT for pending decisions
userStories.governanceImpact: Supports human-in-the-loop risk register workflows.

userStories.title: Warehouse DDL — dashboard_snapshots table
userStories.description: As an executive stakeholder, I want point-in-time dashboard snapshots stored so KPI trends can be compared across audit periods.
acceptanceCriteria: schemas/analytics_warehouse.sql adds dashboard_snapshots (snapshot_id, captured_at, kpi_json, source_audit_dir, hash_chain_valid)
testPlan: tests/schemas/test_warehouse_ddl.py asserts dashboard_snapshots table
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Revert DDL; snapshots table unused until loader implemented.
auditRequirements: kpi_json stored as TEXT (JSON); hash_chain_valid BOOLEAN for tamper awareness
source_audit_dir records provenance path, not file contents
userStories.governanceImpact: Enables audit-period KPI comparison for committees.

userStories.title: Warehouse migration runner script
userStories.description: As a platform engineer, I want a repeatable migration script so warehouse schema can be applied to local SQLite or portfolio demo DB without manual SQL.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/warehouse_migrate.py applies schemas/analytics_warehouse.sql to --db path (default: data/analytics_warehouse.db)
Idempotent: CREATE IF NOT EXISTS only; safe to re-run
CLI prints applied statement count and final schema version marker
testPlan: tests/scripts/test_warehouse_migrate.py — temp DB, run twice, assert tables exist
Assert second run does not error
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Delete target DB file or run from backup; script is additive only.
auditRequirements: Script must not delete or truncate existing tables
Log migration timestamp to stdout for operator audit
userStories.riskImpact: Low — local/portfolio DB only by default.
userStories.governanceImpact: Reproducible schema for demo and CI environments.

userStories.title: JSONL audit to warehouse loader
userStories.description: As a data analyst, I want to load hash-chained audit JSONL into the analytics warehouse so incidents, evidence, and control tests are queryable via SQL.
acceptanceCriteria: Maps analytics pipeline output to incidents, evidence_events, control_tests rows
CLI subcommand or documented invocation in README analytics section
testPlan: tests/scripts/test_warehouse_load_audit.py with tests/fixtures/risk_analytics/audit_sample
Assert row counts match expected fixture manifest
Re-run loader; assert no duplicate primary keys
userStories.rollbackStrategy: DELETE FROM affected tables for loaded audit_dir or restore DB backup; loader does not modify source JSONL.
auditRequirements: Preserve hash_chain_valid flag from source audit metadata when available
Log load batch_id and row counts to stdout
Never modify source JSONL files
userStories.riskImpact: Medium — incorrect mapping could misrepresent incident KPIs; mitigated by fixture tests.
userStories.governanceImpact: Enables SQL-based governance reporting without altering evidence chain.

userStories.title: EvidenceEvent shim between evidence_schema and platform_core
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a compatibility shim so windows_network_toolkit EvidenceEvent and platform_core evidence models convert bidirectionally without duplicate normalizers.
acceptanceCriteria: Preserves tier (T0–T5), signal_type, limitation, and event_id
No changes to existing public CLI output schemas
testPlan: tests/platform_kernel/test_evidence_shim.py — round-trip on fixture events
Assert unknown fields do not raise; stored in raw_data or dropped per documented rules
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Remove shim module; callers continue using evidence_schema directly.
auditRequirements: Shim must not upgrade claim_strength during conversion
Log dropped fields at DEBUG only, not in audit JSONL
userStories.governanceImpact: Reduces dual-model drift that could cause inconsistent proof tiers.

userStories.title: Incident classifier adapter in platform_kernel
userStories.description: As a risk analyst, I want classify_incidents exposed through platform_kernel so API and CLI use the same incident_class and risk_rating mapping.
acceptanceCriteria: Returns list of IncidentRecord-compatible dicts with standard limitations block
Includes proxy_health_status and reverter_suspected when present in events
testPlan: tests/platform_kernel/test_classifier.py — parity with tests/windows_network_toolkit/test_analytics_pipeline.py incident labels
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Remove classifier.py; analytics_pipeline unchanged.
auditRequirements: Every IncidentRecord includes limitations[] citing correlation≠causation where listener attribution used
No malware or threat-actor labels in output
userStories.governanceImpact: Consistent incident taxonomy for committee reporting.

userStories.title: PROXY_REVERTER_DETECTION control test hardening
userStories.description: As a control owner, I want PROXY_REVERTER_DETECTION control tests to have explicit edge-case coverage for flapping and insufficient-history scenarios.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/windows_network_toolkit/test_control_tests.py adds cases: flapping history, single-sample insufficient, clean disable
map_control_tests_from_incident returns NOT_TESTED or INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE when history < 2 transitions
README control tests section mentions PROXY_REVERTER_DETECTION limitations
testPlan: pytest tests/windows_network_toolkit/test_control_tests.py -k REVERTER
Fixture: tests/fixtures/proxy_watch_localhost.json
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Revert test additions and README; production control_tests.py behavior unchanged if only tests added.
auditRequirements: Control test output must state evidence window size used
FAIL requires explicit criterion, not heuristic malware inference
userStories.governanceImpact: Clearer control maturity signals for proxy reverter incidents.

userStories.title: Proxy health JSON schema stability contract
userStories.description: As an integrator, I want proxy-health CLI JSON output to follow a versioned schema so downstream monitors do not break on patch releases.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/windows_network_toolkit/test_proxy_health.py asserts required keys: status, probes, limitations, listener
Breaking changes require schema_version bump documented in CHANGELOG or README
testPlan: pytest tests/windows_network_toolkit/test_proxy_health.py -k schema
Compare against tests/fixtures/proxy_health_dead.json structure
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Remove schema_version field if consumers not ready; keep required keys test.
auditRequirements: status enum values documented: HEALTHY, DEAD, LISTENER_NOT_PROXY, DIRECT_ONLY_WORKS, UNKNOWN
limitations[] always present in JSON output
userStories.governanceImpact: Stable health contract for read-only monitoring integrations.

userStories.title: Risk API v1 router scaffold
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I want a versioned /v1/risk API namespace so executive dashboard endpoints are isolated from legacy /platform/* routes.
acceptanceCriteria: Mounted in backend/main.py without breaking existing routes
testPlan: tests/backend/test_risk_v1_routes.py — TestClient health endpoint
Assert existing /platform/decision routes still respond
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Remove router mount from main.py and delete risk_v1_routes.py.
auditRequirements: Health endpoint is read-only
No auth bypass introduced — document auth requirements in OpenAPI
userStories.governanceImpact: Clean API surface for risk analytics consumers.

userStories.title: GET /v1/risk/summary endpoint
userStories.description: As an executive viewer, I want a summary endpoint returning KPI rollups from the analytics pipeline so the dashboard can render without client-side JSONL parsing.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /v1/risk/summary returns build_dashboard_dataset() KPIs as JSON
Supports ?fixture= path for demo mode and ?audit_dir= for live ingest (read-only)
Response includes limitations[] and generated_at timestamp
testPlan: tests/backend/test_risk_v1_summary.py — fixture mode matches analytics-summary --json output keys
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Remove endpoint handler; CLI analytics-summary remains available.
auditRequirements: audit_dir parameter must reject paths outside allowed data/ and tests/fixtures/ roots
Response must not include raw registry values or secrets
userStories.riskImpact: Medium — path traversal if audit_dir not sandboxed; mitigated by path allowlist.
userStories.governanceImpact: Executive KPIs served from same pipeline as CLI for consistency.

userStories.title: GET /v1/risk/incidents endpoint with pagination
userStories.description: As a risk analyst, I want paginated incident records from the pipeline so large audit directories do not overwhelm the browser.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /v1/risk/incidents supports limit (default 50, max 200) and offset
Returns {items, total, limit, offset} with IncidentRecord fields
Sorted by incident severity then created_at descending
testPlan: tests/backend/test_risk_v1_incidents.py — pagination boundaries, empty audit dir
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Remove endpoint; no data mutation.
auditRequirements: Each item includes incident_class, risk_rating, proof_tier, limitations
No PII beyond endpoint_id already in audit fixtures
userStories.governanceImpact: Scalable incident review for human analysts.

userStories.title: GET /v1/risk/control-tests endpoint
userStories.description: As a control owner, I want control test results per incident exposed via API for dashboard heatmaps and Power BI live connections.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /v1/risk/control-tests returns flat list of control test results
testPlan: tests/backend/test_risk_v1_control_tests.py — filter by incident_id, assert PROXY_REVERTER_DETECTION present in fixture
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Remove endpoint handler only.
auditRequirements: status values match control_tests warehouse enum
limitations propagated from map_control_tests_from_incident
userStories.governanceImpact: API access to control maturity metrics.

userStories.title: Wire platform_kernel pipeline to windows_network_toolkit CLI
userStories.description: As a CLI user, I want analytics-summary and evidence-report --analytics to optionally use platform_kernel orchestrator so behavior matches the risk API.
acceptanceCriteria: Default path unchanged (backward compatible) when flag omitted
Help text documents --kernel as preview/unified path
testPlan: tests/windows_network_toolkit/test_cli_kernel_flag.py — output parity with default on fixture
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Remove --kernel flag; default CLI paths unaffected.
auditRequirements: Flag is read-only; no change to proxy-disable or remediation commands
Deprecation not enabled in this story
userStories.governanceImpact: CLI/API convergence without breaking existing scripts.

userStories.title: Executive risk dashboard route scaffold
userStories.description: As an executive user, I want a dedicated /risk/executive page so technology risk KPIs are separated from operator-focused /platform views.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
testPlan: frontend: manual or playwright smoke if present
Backend running with fixture query param for local demo
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Delete frontend/app/risk/ directory.
auditRequirements: Page displays limitations disclaimer banner
No remediation action buttons on executive page
userStories.governanceImpact: Executive-appropriate view without execution controls.

userStories.title: Executive KPI cards component
userStories.description: As an executive user, I want KPI cards (incident count, control pass rate, high-risk count) on the executive dashboard so I can assess posture at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: KPI cards render total_incidents, control_pass_rate, high_risk_incidents from summary API
Responsive grid for desktop and tablet widths
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
testPlan: Visual check against tests/fixtures/analytics_pipeline_fixture.json expected KPIs
Component unit test if project uses React Testing Library
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Revert component; page shell remains.
auditRequirements: KPI definitions match docs/analytics_data_model.md
Tooltip or footnote cites data source (audit_dir/fixture)
userStories.governanceImpact: Consistent executive metrics definitions.

userStories.title: Incident classification breakdown chart
userStories.description: As a risk committee member, I want a breakdown chart of incident classes so I can see whether proxy drift dominates the portfolio.
acceptanceCriteria: Chart displays incident_class counts from /v1/risk/summary or incidents aggregate
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
testPlan: Browser verify with analytics_pipeline_fixture
Assert DEAD_PROXY_CONFIG and similar labels appear per fixture
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Remove chart component from executive page.
auditRequirements: Chart title includes 'Classification — not accusation' subtitle or footnote
No threat actor or malware categories displayed
userStories.governanceImpact: Supports committee narrative without over-claiming causation.

userStories.title: Control test status heatmap
userStories.description: As a control owner, I want a heatmap of control_id × status so I can spot systematically failing controls across incidents.
acceptanceCriteria: Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
testPlan: Browser verify with fixture data
API test data includes at least 2 control_ids
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Remove heatmap component.
auditRequirements: Tooltip shows evidence_tier and limitations
FAIL cells do not imply security incident without human review flag
userStories.governanceImpact: Control maturity visibility for GRC stakeholders.

userStories.title: Dashboard snapshot export to warehouse
userStories.description: As an auditor, I want executive dashboard KPIs snapshotted into dashboard_snapshots after each analytics-export so point-in-time committee reports are reproducible.
acceptanceCriteria: analytics-export --snapshot-db path writes row to dashboard_snapshots
Snapshot includes kpi_json from build_dashboard_dataset and hash_chain_valid from audit ingest
Documented in README Evidence to analytics pipeline section
testPlan: tests/windows_network_toolkit/test_analytics_snapshot.py — export then query SQLite for snapshot row
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Remove --snapshot-db flag; analytics-export CSV behavior unchanged.
auditRequirements: Each snapshot records source_audit_dir and captured_at UTC
Snapshots are append-only; no update of prior snapshot_id
userStories.governanceImpact: Reproducible committee reporting artifacts.

userStories.title: Deprecate legacy analytics-summary platform path
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want --legacy-platform flag documented as deprecated with a stderr warning so users migrate to the unified kernel pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: analytics-summary --legacy-platform emits DeprecationWarning or stderr notice pointing to default/--kernel path
tests/test_analytics_summary.py still passes for legacy path
README notes deprecation timeline (next minor release)
testPlan: pytest tests/test_analytics_summary.py
Assert warning captured in test when legacy flag used
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Remove warning text; legacy flag behavior unchanged.
auditRequirements: Deprecation must not remove legacy path in this story
Warning cites governance reason: single pipeline for audit consistency
userStories.governanceImpact: Reduces dual-pipeline audit divergence over time.

userStories.title: CI workflow — Ralph story verification job
userStories.description: As a release engineer, I want CI to validate prd.json schema and run pytest markers for completed stories so autonomous merges do not skip verification.
acceptanceCriteria: validate_prd.py checks required fields on each userStory and priority ordering
Fails if duplicate story IDs or non-monotonic priority with dependency violations
testPlan: tests/scripts/test_validate_prd.py — valid prd passes, intentionally broken prd fails
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Remove CI step and validate_prd.py.
auditRequirements: CI logs story count and branchName
No secrets in prd.json validation output
userStories.governanceImpact: Enforces story quality for autonomous engineering loops.

userStories.title: platform_kernel documentation and README integration
userStories.description: As a new contributor, I want platform_kernel documented in README and docs/architecture.md so the unified pipeline location is discoverable.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/platform-kernel.md describes run_incident_pipeline, models, and safety boundaries
README architecture section links to platform_kernel and warehouse loader
Mermaid or text diagram shows Evidence → Classify → Control tests → Analytics → Warehouse
testPlan: Manual: follow README commands end-to-end on fixture
Link check: no broken relative links
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Revert doc files only.
auditRequirements: Docs must state observation≠proof and dry-run default
No documentation implying autonomous remediation
userStories.governanceImpact: Onboarding aligned with governance model.

userStories.title: End-to-end Ralph demo script
userStories.description: As a portfolio reviewer, I want a single scripts/ralph_demo.ps1 that runs the full risk pipeline, loads warehouse, and prints API URLs so Ralph iterations can smoke-test the platform.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/ralph_demo.ps1 runs: warehouse_migrate, analytics-export with fixture, warehouse_load_audit, curl /v1/risk/summary
Exits non-zero on any step failure
docs/demo-script.md references ralph_demo.ps1 as optional automation path
testPlan: Run scripts/ralph_demo.ps1 locally in CI-compatible mode
Optional: tests/scripts/test_ralph_demo.py mocks subprocess calls
userStories.rollbackStrategy: Delete script; manual demo steps in docs/demo-script.md remain.
auditRequirements: Script uses tests/fixtures only by default
Prints limitations disclaimer before KPI output
userStories.governanceImpact: Repeatable portfolio demonstration of governance pipeline.
